iti74:1.2: This was said by the Blessed One, said by the Arahant, so I have heard:
iti74:1.3: "There are these three types of sons and daughters existing in the world. Which three? One of heightened birth, one of similar birth, one of lowered birth.
iti74:1.4: "And how is a son or daughter of heightened birth? There is the case where a son or daughter's parents have not gone to the Buddha for refuge, have not gone to the Dhamma for refuge, have not gone to the Saṅgha for refuge. They do not abstain from taking life, from stealing, from sexual misconduct, from false speech, from fermented and distilled liquors that cause heedlessness. They are unprincipled and evil by nature. However, their son or daughter has gone to the Buddha for refuge, has gone to the Dhamma for refuge, has gone to the Saṅgha for refuge. He/she abstains from taking life, from stealing, from sexual misconduct, from false speech, from fermented and distilled liquors that cause heedlessness. He/she is principled and admirable by nature. This is called a son or daughter of heightened birth.
iti74:1.5: "And how is a son or daughter of similar birth? There is the case where a son or daughter's parents have gone to the Buddha for refuge, have gone to the Dhamma for refuge, have gone to the Saṅgha for refuge. They abstain from taking life, from stealing, from sexual misconduct, from false speech, from fermented and distilled liquors that cause heedlessness. They are principled and admirable by nature. Their son or daughter has also gone to the Buddha for refuge, has gone to the Dhamma for refuge, has gone to the Saṅgha for refuge. He/she abstains from taking life, from stealing, from sexual misconduct, from false speech, from fermented and distilled liquors that cause heedlessness. He/she is principled and admirable by nature. This is called a son or daughter of similar birth.
iti74:1.6: "And how is a son or daughter of lowered birth? There is the case where a son or daughter's parents have gone to the Buddha for refuge, have gone to the Dhamma for refuge, have gone to the Saṅgha for refuge. They abstain from taking life, from stealing, from sexual misconduct, from false speech, from fermented and distilled liquors that cause heedlessness. They are principled and admirable by nature. However, their son or daughter has not gone to the Buddha for refuge, has not gone to the Dhamma for refuge, has not gone to the Saṅgha for refuge. He/she does not abstain from taking life, from stealing, from sexual misconduct, from false speech, from fermented and distilled liquors that cause heedlessness. He/she is unprincipled and evil by nature. This is called a son or daughter of lowered birth."
iti74:1.7: The wise hope for a child of heightened or similar birth, not for one of lowered birth, a disgrace to the family.
iti74:1.8: These children in the world, lay followers, consummate in virtue, conviction; generous, free from stinginess, shine forth in any gathering like the moon when freed from a cloud.
iti74:1.9: This, too, was the meaning of what was said by the Blessed One, so I have heard.
